name: Thundrik's Profiteers
inspire: After you score an Objective card, if a friendly leader ({icon:leader}) is on the battlefield, you can pick a friendly fighter. Inspire that fighter.

reactions.name: Atmospheric Isolation
reactions.trigger: Use this immediately after your Action step if a friendly leader ({icon:leader}) used any Core abilities in that step.
reactions.flavorText: At the push of a button, Thundrik creates a vacuum to suffocate his foes.
reactions.rulesText: Until the end of the next Action step, each time an enemy fighter is placed in, is pushed into or enters a hex adjacent to that leader ({icon:leader}), inflict 1 damage on that fighter.

reactions.name: Custom Aethershot Rounds
reactions.trigger: Use this immediately after you pick a ranged weapon as part of an Attack.
reactions.flavorText: They don't come carved with your enemy's name on the casings, but these rounds are no less deadly for it.
reactions.rulesText: That weapon has +1 Range and +1 Attack dice for that Attack. You can only use this ability once per game.

reactions.name: Hail of Aethershot
reactions.trigger: Use this immediately after a friendly fighter's ranged Attack if they have no Move or Charge tokens.
reactions.flavorText: Volume of fire solves most problems.
reactions.rulesText: That fighter Attacks again using the same weapon. The target of that Attack must be different to the target of the first Attack and must be within 2 hexes of the first target. You can only use this ability once per game.

abilities.name: 'This is Mine, Fair and Square'
abilities.flavorText: The Kharadron have no time for non-binding contracts.
abilities.rulesText: While a friendly fighter is holding a treasure token and there are no enemy fighters adjacent to them, that fighter cannot be pushed by enemy abilities.

abilities.name: By the Code
abilities.flavorText: The Kharadron Code grants the duardin leeway to pursue their goals, provided lawful profits are earned and taxes duly paid.
abilities.rulesText: Use this in a Power step. Pick a friendly fighter. Push that fighter up to 3 hexes. That push must end on a feature token. You can only use this ability once per game.
